name: Cuerda del Pozo Reservoir Loop
name_original: Embalse de la Cuerda del Pozo – circular desde Playa Pita
region: Embalse de la Cuerda del Pozo
description: This moderate 16.1-mile mountain biking route circles the Embalse de la Cuerda del Pozo from Playa Pita, offering scenic views.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Embalse de la Cuerda del Pozo features varied terrain, from paths along the reservoir's 65 kilometers of coastline to routes through dense pine forests. The region is characterized by its natural environment, with mountains like Urbión providing a scenic backdrop. Riders can expect a mix of flat sections near the water and more undulating paths as they venture into the surrounding woodlands. Best mountain bike trails around Embalse de la Cuerda del Pozo The most popular mountain bike trail is Cuerda del Pozo Reservoir Loop, a 16.1 miles (25.9 km) trail that takes 2 hours 4 minutes to complete, offering scenic views around the reservoir. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Molinos de Duero – Bridge over the Duero loop from Embalse de la Cuerda del Pozo, an easy 13.4 miles (21.5 km) path that crosses the Duero river and provides a gentle ride through the landscape. Local mountain bikers also love the Ermita de Santa Elena – Sotogrande Shelter loop from Restaurante Urbión, a 24.8 miles (39.9 km) trail leading through pine forests and offering views of the reservoir, often completed in about 3 hours 28 minutes. Mountain biking around Embalse de la Cuerda del Pozo is defined by routes along the reservoir's shore, through pine forests, and with views of the surrounding mountains. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy loops to more challenging paths. The routes in Embalse de la Cuerda del Pozo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.9 stars from more than 20 reviews. More than 700 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Embalse de la Cuerda del Pozo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of mountain bike trails can I find around Embalse de la Cuerda del Pozo?
A: The area offers a diverse range of mountain bike trails, from paths along the reservoir's 65 kilometers of coastline to routes winding through dense pine forests. You'll find a mix of easy, moderate, and challenging options, with terrain varying from flat sections near the water to more undulating paths in the woodlands. There are 50 mountain bike routes available, including 9 easy, 29 moderate, and 12 difficult trails.

Q: Are there any mountain bike routes suitable for beginners or families with children?
A: Yes, Embalse de la Cuerda del Pozo has several routes suitable for less experienced riders and families. There are 9 easy mountain bike trails in the region. An example of a gentle ride is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38195359" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Molinos de Duero – Bridge over the Duero loop from Embalse de la Cuerda del Pozo&lt;/a&gt;, which is 21.5 km long and crosses the Duero river.

Q: What are some of the more challenging mountain bike trails in the area?
A: For experienced mountain bikers seeking a challenge, there are 12 difficult trails around Embalse de la Cuerda del Pozo. A notable option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38188062" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cuerda del Pozo Reservoir – Bridge over the Duero loop from Playa Pita&lt;/a&gt;, which spans 62 km and features over 1000 meters of elevation gain.

Q: Can I find circular mountain bike routes around the reservoir?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike routes around Embalse de la Cuerda del Pozo are circular, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1056730323/cuerda-del-pozo-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cuerda del Pozo Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a popular 25.9 km circular trail offering scenic views of the water.

Q: What natural features or points of interest can I see while mountain biking?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty and historical intrigue. You'll ride through lush pine forests and enjoy views of the Urbión mountains. Keep an eye out for the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5572169" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cuerda del Pozo Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; itself, and if water levels are low, you might spot the bell tower of the submerged village of La Muedra or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2721569" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Submerged Roman Bridge of Vinuesa&lt;/a&gt;. Popular spots like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5315870" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Playa Pita&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3499185" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Playa Gamella&lt;/a&gt; also offer scenic stops.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in this region?
A: The spring and autumn months are generally ideal for mountain biking around Embalse de la Cuerda del Pozo, offering pleasant temperatures and vibrant landscapes. While summer is also popular, especially for water activities, it can be warmer. Winter riding is possible, but be prepared for colder conditions and potentially more challenging terrain.

Q: Are there places to park near the mountain bike trails?
A: Yes, parking is generally available at key access points around the reservoir, such as near Playa Pita or in villages like Molinos de Duero and Vinuesa, which serve as common starting points for many routes. Always check local signage for specific parking regulations.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails here?
A: The mountain bike trails around Embalse de la Cuerda del Pozo are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.9 stars from over 20 reviews. Riders often praise the varied terrain, the stunning reservoir views, and the peaceful atmosphere of the pine forests.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the mountain bike trails?
A: Many trails in natural areas like Embalse de la Cuerda del Pozo are dog-friendly, especially if your dog is well-behaved and kept on a leash. However, it's always advisable to check specific trail regulations or local signage, particularly in protected areas, to ensure dogs are permitted.

Q: Are there any long-distance mountain bike routes for extended rides?
A: Yes, for those looking for longer rides, the region offers routes that can be combined or are inherently long. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20151048" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ermita de La Blanca – Pinar Grande Station loop from Restaurante Urbión&lt;/a&gt; is a substantial 47.6 km route through forests, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38188062" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cuerda del Pozo Reservoir – Bridge over the Duero loop from Playa Pita&lt;/a&gt; covers 62 km, providing a full day's adventure.

Q: Are there facilities like cafes or restaurants near the mountain bike routes?
A: Yes, you'll find amenities in the villages surrounding the reservoir, such as Molinos de Duero and Vinuesa. Playa Pita, a central hub for activities, also features a bar-restaurant. These spots are convenient for refreshments or a meal before or after your ride.
tips: Beautiful views of the Cuerda del Pozo reservoir from Playa Pita, where we will find a parking lot and a recreational area with various services, where we can take a bath and eat.
Beautiful views over the Duero river to the town of Vinuesa from the Los Carbonales viewpoint. We can access by car, since in the same place we will find a parking lot.

If the water level is high we will not be able to see the Old Roman Bridge of Vinuesa that is hidden under the water. It is worth going when the water level allows us to appreciate such a wonder surrounded by nature.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to mountain bike this circular route?
faqs.answer: The route is approximately 25.9 kilometers long and generally takes around 2 hours and 4 minutes to complete. This duration is an estimate and can vary based on your pace and stops.

faqs.question: Is this mountain bike trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: This trail is classified as moderate. While it offers a picturesque experience, some segments are unpaved and may present challenges. Cyclists should possess good physical fitness and some cycling experience to comfortably tackle the route.

faqs.question: Where does the trail start and where can I park?
faqs.answer: The circular route starts from Playa Pita. Playa Pita is a unique inland beach that offers recreational facilities, including ample parking, making it a convenient starting point for your ride.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: The terrain primarily consists of paths and tracks, with some unpaved segments. You'll ride through diverse landscapes including pine forests, mountains, and oak groves, offering a varied and engaging experience.

faqs.question: What notable sights or features can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: Along the route, you'll enjoy beautiful views of the Cuerda del Pozo Reservoir, especially from Playa Pita. You'll also get a View of Vinuesa and pass by the Ancient Roman Bridge of Vinuesa. A unique historical element is the submerged bell tower of the old village of La Muedra, which can be seen when water levels in the reservoir are lower.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike the Embalse de la Cuerda del Pozo circular route?
faqs.answer: The region is beautiful throughout the year, but spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for mountain biking. Summer can be warm, so early mornings or late afternoons are advisable, while winter rides might require appropriate gear for colder conditions.

faqs.question: Is the Embalse de la Cuerda del Pozo circular route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the area around the Embalse de la Cuerda del Pozo and Playa Pita is generally dog-friendly. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or other visitors, and to clean up after them.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this mountain bike trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to access and ride this mountain bike trail around the Embalse de la Cuerda del Pozo.

faqs.question: Are there facilities like cafes or water sources near the trail?
faqs.answer: Playa Pita, the starting point, offers recreational facilities. For more extensive options like cafes and pubs, you would typically find them in nearby towns such as Vinuesa, Molinos de Duero, or Abejar, which are a short distance from the reservoir area.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route overlap with other official trails. Approximately 14% of the route follows parts of the 'Etapa 01: Fuentes del Duero – Vinuesa' and another 14% runs along the 'Ruta del Duero'.

faqs.question: What's an insider tip for mountain biking this route?
faqs.answer: Keep an eye out for the historic submerged bell tower of the old village of La Muedra. It's a fascinating sight that becomes visible when the water levels of the Cuerda del Pozo Reservoir are lower, adding a unique historical touch to your ride.
